meaning: n.赞美,骑士爵位的授予,连谱号
examples: The Nobel Prize has become the ultimate accolade in the sciences.
The accolade, awarded by the magazaine Total Politics, goes to the bizarre video cover of the Bucks Fizz classic "Making your mind up".
Strangely, the yen may be the more deserving of that accolade.
"This double accolade is uniquely deserved, " said Sir Peter Stothard, chairman of the judges.
He introduced me to the crowd as a great karate master, an accolade I didn't merit.
